varieties.name: Chunpoong
varieties.traits: Premium red ginseng quality, good root shape, long taproot
varieties.growing_notes: 22.3% Chun-Jeesam (first-grade) vs 9.4% for Jakyungjong Highly resistant to rusty root rot Cold temperate with frost Most cultivated variety in South Korea Excellent for red ginseng production

varieties.name: Yunpoong
varieties.traits: High yield, early flowering, very thick root diameter

varieties.name: Gopoong
varieties.origin: Korea (selected from Jakyung, same breeding as Chunpoong/Yunpoong)
varieties.traits: Highest ginsenoside content among varieties
varieties.growing_notes: Superior saponin profile Cold temperate Primarily for red ginseng manufacture Highest unit content 18.9mg/g and total content 596mg/root

varieties.name: Gumpoong
varieties.traits: Yellow berries (distinctive), disease resistant
varieties.growing_notes: Highly tolerant to rusty roots (1.29-1.45 rating vs 2.45-2.60)

varieties.name: Sunpoong
varieties.traits: Long peduncle, combines high yield with excellent quality
varieties.growing_notes: 20.9% heaven-and-earth grade (vs 9.4% for Yunpoong) Cold temperate

varieties.name: Sunone
varieties.traits: Root rot resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Highly resistant to root rot (44.4% survival vs 21.7% for landrace) Cold temperate For problematic soils with disease pressure Root rot tolerance

varieties.name: Sunun
varieties.traits: First cultivar with strong leaf surface blistering
varieties.growing_notes: Cold temperate Strong blistering on leaflet surface

varieties.name: Sunhyang
varieties.traits: High Aroma-1 content, unique red ginseng scent
varieties.growing_notes: Cold temperate Premium red ginseng with enhanced aroma Aromatic properties for processed ginseng

varieties.name: Cheongsun (Chungsun)
varieties.traits: Selected breeding line, commercial cultivar

varieties.name: K-1
varieties.traits: Modern breeding selection; Good root shape, productive lateral roots, disease resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Effective against multiple pathogens Cold temperate

varieties.name: G-1
varieties.traits: Recent breeding program release; Recent selection with SNP markers

varieties.name: Jakyung (Jakyungjong)
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace, foundation genetics; Violet stems, red fruits, historic baseline variety

varieties.name: Hwangsook
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace; Historic variety, foundation genetics

varieties.name: Jilin Huangguo Renshen
varieties.traits: Higher ginsenoside content than local landraces

varieties.name: Damaya
varieties.traits: Higher medicinal and commercial value, premium quality

varieties.name: Ermaya
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace with specific terroir; Specific environmental adaptation to river basin

varieties.name: Biantiao (Biaotiao)
varieties.traits: Beautiful humanoid root shape, long main root, branch root
varieties.growing_notes: Premium ornamental-quality medicinal root Cold temperate

varieties.name: Xinkaihe 01
varieties.traits: Bred specifically for high-quality Biantiao ginseng production
varieties.growing_notes: Specialized for premium root morphology Cold temperate

varieties.name: Xinkaihe 02
varieties.traits: Shorter rhizome than Xinkaihe 01
varieties.growing_notes: Cold temperate Suitable for model red ginseng production

varieties.name: Jishen 01
varieties.traits: Heavy root weight, long main root, good shape

varieties.name: Fuxing 01
varieties.traits: High yield, strong disease resistance

varieties.name: Changbo
varieties.traits: Historic variety; Traditional variety, phenotypically distinct

varieties.name: Huangguo
varieties.traits: Heirloom wild species, 250+ years cultivation; Traditional variety, part of Chinese ginseng diversity

varieties.name: No formal cultivar breeding programs - grown from wild-collected seed
varieties.origin: Northeastern Asia (Siberia, Far East Russia, China, Korea, Japan)
varieties.traits: Heirloom wild species, traditional use since 1950s medicinal research

varieties.name: Adaptogenic properties similar to Panax
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries list it but note unsuitable for hot climates. Frost resistant but drought tender. Prefers filtered light in woodland setting.

varieties.name: Trichopus zeylanicus (Arogyapacha - "Kerala Ginseng")
varieties.traits: 🌏 Indigenous heirloom, centuries of traditional use

varieties.name: Less ideal than native Kerala habitat
Withania somnifera (Ashwagandha - "Indian Ginseng")
varieties.origin: India, Middle East, North Africa, Mediterranean (DRY regions)
varieties.traits: Heirloom species, 5,000+ years traditional use in Ayurveda
